title: Is beta(1/2,2) equal to 1/50 in the density version of Turan's theorem?
micro_paper_assessment: A real theorem slice was proved for the C5-homomorphic family, but the global half-set threshold remains unresolved, so this run is not yet paper-ready in the micro-paper lane.
family_affinity: Strong for balanced C5 blowups and C5-homomorphic triangle-free graphs; unresolved for Petersen-type witnesses and general triangle-free structure.
generalization_signal: The weighted-template averaging argument should transfer to Petersen blowups or a future stability reduction, and nearby alpha values may be tractable inside fixed five-part families.
candidate_theorem_slice: Among triangle-free graphs admitting a homomorphism to C5, the maximum possible minimum induced edge density over n/2-vertex subsets is 1/50, achieved uniquely by the balanced C5 blowup.
next_action: Verify the weighted C5 optimization and equality case, then attempt the same half-set optimization on Petersen blowups or prove a reduction from near-extremal graphs to the known witness families.
